manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<BOS_TOKEN><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|USER_TOKEN|>You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 mirrors, 1 brick, 3 knives, 1 comb

Your secret value function is:

mirror: 1, brick: 4, knife: 1, comb: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.<|END_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|CHATBOT_TOKEN|>

raw_response_obj.response:
<BOS_TOKEN><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|USER_TOKEN|>You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 mirrors, 1 brick, 3 knives, 1 comb

Your secret value function is:

mirror: 1, brick: 4, knife: 1, comb: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.<|END_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|START_OF_TURN_TOKEN|><|CHATBOT_TOKEN|>Understanding the goal is to maximize the total score, I'd like to initiate a conversation with a proposal that could benefit both of us. Given our limited communication messages, I'll suggest a fair division based on our maximum utility.

Message 1: I propose we each take one mirror, as they seem to hold the least value for both of us compared to other items.

Message 2: The brick appears to be the most valuable item. I'm willing to concede this to you if you can agree to take one of the knives and the comb as well. This leaves me with one knife, which is my next most valued item.

Message 3: Assuming you're okay with the division of the brick, knives, and comb, I'd like to confirm our agreement. Please confirm with a simple 'agreed' if you're on board.

Message 4: If there are any adjustments you'd like to make, please let me know. I'm open to discussing alternatives if you have a different idea.

Message 5: If I don't hear back from you, I'll assume we're on the same page and submit my proposal.

**Secret Proposal:** [Proposal: 1 mirror, 1 knife]

This division allows for a fair distribution based on our value functions and ensures a positive score for both of us.<|END_OF_TURN_TOKEN|>

clem_player.response:
Based on our goal to maximize the sum of our scores, let's make a proposal that caters to both our value functions. Given your values, it seems you highly value the comb and mirrors. Since there are two mirrors and you value them highly, I'll propose we each take one. I'll also suggest that you take the comb, given its high value to you. I, on the other hand, will take the brick and one of the knives (since knives have no value to you).

Secret Proposal: "Proposal: 1 mirror, 1 brick, 1 knife"

This proposal ensures that we both get items of significant value to us, maximizing our combined score. It also leaves room for further negotiation if needed, given we have not used up our message limit.
